machine_learning_fundamentals.question: What is Machine Learning, and how does it differ from traditional programming?
What is the difference between supervised, unsupervised, and reinforcement learning?
What is a dataset?
What are features and labels in a machine learning dataset?
What is the purpose of splitting a dataset into training and testing sets?
What is overfitting?
What is underfitting?
What is the purpose of a machine learning model?
What is the difference between classification and regression?
Why is data quality important in machine learning?
Explain the bias-variance trade-off in machine learning.
How does cross-validation improve model evaluation?
What is feature engineering, and why is it important?
How would you handle missing values in a dataset?
Explain the importance of feature scaling.
How would you deal with an imbalanced dataset?
What evaluation metrics would you use for a classification problem, and why?
Explain the purpose of regularization in machine learning.
How would you determine whether a machine learning model is generalizing well?
Explain the complete machine learning workflow from collecting data to deploying a model.
How would you diagnose whether poor model performance is caused by insufficient data, poor features, or an inappropriate algorithm?
Explain the trade-offs between model complexity, interpretability, and predictive performance.
How would you design a machine learning pipeline capable of training on terabytes of data?
How would you identify and reduce data leakage during model development?
Describe strategies for selecting the most appropriate machine learning algorithm for a new problem.
Explain how hyperparameter tuning improves model performance and compare common optimization techniques.
How would you design a machine learning system that continuously learns from new data without degrading performance?
How would you evaluate whether additional training data is likely to improve a model more than choosing a different algorithm?
Explain how concept drift affects machine learning models deployed in production.
How would you design a robust machine learning pipeline for a safety-critical application?
A machine learning model performs exceptionally well during development but poorly after deployment. Walk me through how you would investigate the issue.
Your customer reports that model predictions have gradually become less accurate over the past six months. Explain how you would determine whether concept drift is responsible.
A teammate claims that collecting more data will solve every machine learning problem. How would you evaluate whether additional data is actually the right solution?
You discover that sensitive customer information has accidentally been included in your training dataset. Describe the actions you would take before continuing model development.
Your team must build a fraud detection model, but only 0.2% of transactions are fraudulent. Explain how you would approach the problem.
A stakeholder requests a highly accurate model, while regulators require that every prediction be explainable. How would you balance these competing requirements?
A newly trained model significantly outperforms the production model during testing. Describe the additional validation you would perform before deployment.
Your team receives a completely new dataset with hundreds of features but little documentation. Walk me through how you would begin developing a machine learning solution.
A deployed recommendation model begins making unusual suggestions after a marketing campaign changes customer behavior. Explain how you would investigate the problem.

You're leading the development of a machine learning system expected to serve millions of users. Describe how you would design the training, evaluation, deployment, and monitoring pipeline to ensure long-term reliability.
